meta_title: For Students: email automation to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Email Automation to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn to build professional email automation projects using Antigravity and Claude Code. Overcome the 'no portfolio' barrier with this beginner-friendly guide.
content.answer_block: Build a functional email automation tool to demonstrate technical proficiency. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid API integration and Claude Code for iterative development, you create a tangible asset that showcases your ability to bridge business logic with software engineering—proving your value to recruiters and clients immediately.
content.intro:
The silence of a blank portfolio is the greatest obstacle a junior developer faces. You have the theoretical knowledge, but employers demand proof of impact. It is a classic catch-22: you need experience to get the job, but you need the job to gain experience. 

Today, we shatter that cycle. We are moving beyond CRUD apps and into the world of high-utility automation. By building an intelligent email automation system, you aren't just writing code—you are solving a universal business problem. This project serves as a beacon, signaling to recruiters that you understand how to automate workflows, manage APIs, and deliver actual business value.

steps.title: Define the Automation Logic
steps.description: Identify a specific problem to solve, such as automated weekly newsletter summaries or client lead follow-ups, to ensure the project has clear business utility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Python architecture for an email automation script that fetches data from a mock JSON API and formats it into a professional, HTML-templated email.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a python project with a main.py file and a requirements.txt file for smtplib and jinja2 dependencies.'

steps.title: Configure the Email Engine
steps.description: Set up your SMTP connection to interface with a mail provider, ensuring security and proper authentication practices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure module to handle SMTP connections using environment variables for credentials, ensuring no hardcoded keys exist in the codebase.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement a secure mail_sender.py module that uses python-dotenv to pull SMTP_USER and SMTP_PASS environment variables.'

steps.title: Develop the Templating Layer
steps.description: Use Jinja2 to separate your content from your code, allowing for dynamic, personalized email generation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Jinja2 template for a professional business email that accepts dynamic variables for recipient name and data summaries.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a templates/email_template.html file and add a function in main.py to render it with dummy data.'

steps.title: Implement Scheduling Logic
steps.description: Transform your script from a one-off execution into a background service using basic scheduling techniques.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a scheduling loop using the 'schedule' library to trigger the email automation task every Monday at 9:00 AM.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Add a scheduler loop in main.py that runs the send_email function using the schedule library, ensuring the process stays alive.'

steps.title: Documentation and Deployment
steps.description: Document your project to show you think like a senior engineer, then prepare it for a live portfolio demo.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional README.md that explains the project's purpose, technical stack, environment setup, and how to run the automation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a high-quality README.md including installation steps, usage instructions, and a section on how this automates workflow efficiency.'

pro_tips: Always use environment variables for keys; hardcoding is the fastest way to lose credibility in a code review.
Include a 'test mode' flag in your script that logs emails to the console instead of sending them to avoid spamming.
Use a professional HTML email boilerplate to ensure your messages look consistent across Gmail, Outlook, and Apple Mail.
Add logging functionality using Python's built-in 'logging' module to track errors in production environments.
Write your README as if you are pitching the project to a CTO, focusing on the 'why' and the business impact rather than just the code.
content.next_steps: Once your script is functional, deploy it to a free-tier cloud service like Railway or Render and add a 'Live Demo' link to your GitHub repository to stand out from other candidates.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to build a professional-grade automation tool that proves you can ship real software. Don't let your code sit on your local machine—get it into the wild and start building your reputation. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
